question: A tRNA molecule reads the codon via its ANTICODON loop and carries the amino acid at its:
Among cartilaginous fishes, Torpedo is known for producing electric organs, while Trygon is known for having:
Cnidoblasts (cnidocytes) containing nematocysts in Coelenterata are used for all of the following EXCEPT:
Erythroblastosis foetalis is a serious condition in newborns caused by Rh incompatibility. It occurs when:
options: An Rh-positive mother carries an Rh-negative foetus in any pregnancy
An Rh-negative mother, sensitised in a FIRST Rh-positive pregnancy, produces anti-Rh antibodies that attack the RBCs of a SUBSEQUENT Rh-positive foetus
The foetus has blood group O while the mother has group AB
The ABO antigens of the foetus differ from the mother in both A and B

question: Turner's syndrome has the karyotype 45 XO. Which of the following correctly describes the condition?
options: Males with XXY karyotype, gynecomastia, sterile
Females, sterile with rudimentary ovaries, lacking secondary sexual characters
Males with only one X chromosome, fertile

question: In eukaryotes, THREE different RNA polymerases transcribe different RNA types. Which RNA polymerase transcribes mRNA precursor (hnRNA)?
options: RNA Polymerase III (transcribes tRNA, 5S rRNA, snRNAs)
A single RNA polymerase for all RNA types (prokaryotic pattern)

question: After TWO generations of replication in ¹⁴N medium (Meselson-Stahl), the DNA density distribution was:

In a dicotyledonous stem, the innermost layer of the cortex is called endodermis. The cells of this endodermis are characteristically rich in starch grains, and therefore the endodermis in dicot stems is also referred to as:
Different Bt toxin genes are specific to different insect groups. The genes cryIAc and cryIIAb control which pest in cotton, while cryIAb controls which pest in corn?
options: cryIAc/cryIIAb = corn borer; cryIAb = cotton bollworms
cryIAc/cryIIAb = cotton bollworms; cryIAb = corn borer
Both control the same pest — tobacco budworm
cryIAc/cryIIAb = rice stem borer; cryIAb = cotton bollworm

question: The conducting part of the human respiratory system extends from the external nostrils to the terminal bronchioles. The EXCHANGE (respiratory) part consists of:
Solanum tuberosum (potato) and Solanum melongena (brinjal) are placed in the same genus Solanum. This grouping indicates that these two organisms:
options: Share more common characters than species from different genera
Have identical morphological features in all respects

question: The lungs, liver, and skin assist in excretion in addition to the kidneys. Sweat secreted by sweat glands contains NaCl, small amounts of urea, and lactic acid. The primary function of sweating is body cooling, but it also removes wastes. Sebaceous glands eliminate which substances through sebum?
In gymnosperms, needle-like leaves of conifers are an adaptation to reduce water loss. Which structural features of these leaves serve this purpose?
options: Large surface area and open stomata

question: In the sarcomere, the dark band contains myosin and is called the A-band (anisotropic). The light band contains actin and is called the I-band (isotropic). The central region of the A-band that is NOT overlapped by thin filaments in a resting muscle is called the:
Decomposition rates are influenced by the chemical composition of detritus and environmental conditions. Which of the following detritus types would decompose FASTEST?
options: Detritus rich in lignin and chitin
Detritus rich in nitrogen and soluble sugars, warm and moist conditions
Detritus in cold and anaerobic conditions
Detritus from conifer needles with thick wax coatings

question: The interaction between Balanus and Chthamalus in intertidal zones, studied by Connell, demonstrated competitive exclusion. Balanus (barnacle) dominates and excludes Chthamalus from its zone when both are present. This is evidence for:
options: Competitive exclusion — Balanus is the competitively superior species in that zone
Mutualism between the two barnacle species
Commensalism where Balanus benefits without affecting Chthamalus

question: For the oxidation of tripalmitin (a fat), the RQ is approximately 0.7. This is LOWER than 1 because:
options: Fats contain more oxygen relative to carbon, requiring less O₂
Fats are more reduced (less oxygen per carbon) than carbohydrates, requiring more O₂ to oxidise, so CO₂/O₂ ratio < 1
Fats are completely oxidised in glycolysis, bypassing the Krebs cycle
Fats produce less CO₂ because they have fewer carbon atoms

question: The nucleoside formed when adenine is attached to a sugar is called adenosine. When a phosphate group is esterified to the sugar of a nucleoside, it becomes a nucleotide. The nucleotide formed from guanosine with a phosphate group is called:
Transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a flower on a DIFFERENT plant of the same species is called:

The cell body of a neuron contains organelles, cytoplasm, and characteristic granules called Nissl's granules. The short fibres that branch repeatedly from the cell body and transmit impulses TOWARDS the cell body are called:

Estrogen is synthesised and secreted by growing ovarian follicles. After ovulation, the ruptured Graafian follicle transforms into a structure that secretes mainly progesterone. This structure is the:
Cancer cells differ from normal cells in that they have LOST:
options: Contact inhibition — the property that normally stops cells from proliferating when they contact other cells

question: The arrangement of sepals or petals in a bud in relation to other members of the same whorl is called aestivation. In which type of aestivation does one margin of each member overlap the next one in one direction only?
The slime mould plasmodium spreads over which type of substrate?
options: Decaying twigs and leaves, engulfing organic material

question: In terms of CO₂ concentration and photosynthesis, which of the following is CORRECT?
options: C4 plants saturate at CO₂ levels above 450 µL/L, while C3 saturate at ~360 µL/L
C4 plants saturate at ~360 µL/L, while C3 plants can respond to CO₂ concentrations above 450 µL/L
Both C3 and C4 saturate at the same CO₂ concentration
Neither C3 nor C4 responds to increased CO₂ at high light intensity

question: For very large areas like continents, the Z value (slope of species-area relationship) is steeper. For frugivorous birds and mammals in tropical forests, the Z value is approximately:
Cyclosporin A is an immunosuppressive agent used in organ transplant patients. It is produced by:
The "Bakanae" (foolish seedling) disease of rice is caused by the fungus Gibberella fujikuroi. The scientist who demonstrated that symptoms could be produced by sterile fungal filtrates was:
Lamarck's theory of evolution proposed use/disuse of organs as the mechanism. His classic example was:
options: Giraffes elongating their necks by stretching to reach tall trees, and passing this acquired trait to offspring
Darwin's finches changing beak shapes across generations

question: Mitochondria contain their own genetic material and ribosomes. The ribosomes present in the mitochondrial matrix are of which type?
The organ system responsible for excretion and osmoregulation in Annelida is:
After telophase I and cytokinesis, the two cells formed are called a dyad. The brief stage between meiosis I and meiosis II, during which NO DNA replication occurs, is called:
In co-dominance (IA IB genotype), which phenotype is expressed?
options: Blood group AB — both IA and IB express their own sugar antigens simultaneously on RBC surfaces
Blood group O — both cancel each other out

question: A tertiary follicle develops into a mature Graafian follicle. The primary oocyte within the tertiary follicle completes the first meiotic division producing a large haploid secondary oocyte and a small structure. What is this small structure called?
